---
title: "LRP77base: Dose-response (pooled dose slope) — comparator to LRP77"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP77base is the **no-period-variation companion** to [LRP77](../lrp77/). It is
the identical period-resolved dose-response model — Beta-Binomial conditional
change on word reading ($W$, $N = 79$), $\{G, W_{\text{pre}}, A\}$ adjustment,
cumulative-dose control, subject random intercept — except the dose enters as a
**single pooled slope** $\beta_{\text{dose}}\,z(\text{attend})$ instead of the
partial-pooled per-period slopes $\beta_{\text{dose}[p]}$.

Its sole purpose is the nested **PSIS-LOO** comparison against LRP77
(`scripts/compare_statistical_models.py`): *does letting the dose–gain slope vary
by period improve predictive fit?* Given the weak Phase-1 dose structure, LRP77
is expected to shrink toward this model and LOO to be near-indistinguishable; LOO
is read cautiously at this $n$ (the LRP68 caveat). See the LRP77 report for the
full estimand, the locked-DAG adjustment set, the no-`ability -> dose`
assumption, the $G = 2 - \text{group}$ coding (G=1 = intervention), and the expectations caveat.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Pooled dose slope

```{python}
# | echo: false
dose = pd.read_csv(_here / "dose_slope_summary.csv")
dose
```

`dose_pooled` is the single dose slope on the logit scale per 1 SD of per-period
dose; `p_pos` is $P(\text{slope} > 0)$.

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
